name: Augie's Mussel House and Beer Garden

notes: All 69 frozen rows were reconciled exactly once: 43 exact, 3 normalized, 8 variant, 6 extraction artifacts, and 9 Annapolis-only location mismatches.

The live restaurant-issued Alexandria menu publishes a materially larger current food and nonalcoholic catalog, so a complete deterministic rebuild is required before the source item count can be pinned.

Post-repair recheck pinned 122 unique current Alexandria food and nonalcoholic products across 12 ordered sections from 104 Toast products plus 19 owner-only products, with one duplicate Avocado presentation consolidated.
All 69 frozen rows remain reconciled exactly once: 43 exact, 3 normalized, 8 variant, 6 artifacts, and 9 Annapolis-only location mismatches.

The restaurant-issued Alexandria menu says Augie's is not a gluten-free kitchen and asks guests to notify their server; this is a restaurant-wide gluten cross-contact warning, not a complete allergen matrix or a wheat claim.

The restaurant-linked Alexandria Toast catalog supplies partial positive labels for Cheese Fries, Fried Mozzarella, Steak & Cheese Eggrolls, Tuna Tartar, Chicken Sliders, Hummus Dip, and Pretzels & Beer Cheese; those labels remain vendor provenance and missing labels are not negative claims.
Every current row carries may-contain gluten from the restaurant-issued kitchen-wide warning; this is not translated into wheat or any other cross-contact claim.

68 rows have restaurant-issued direct positive ingredient signals, 14 have only the official global warning, 7 preserve explicit restaurant-linked Toast allergen labels, 5 preserve unavoidable restaurant-linked product identities, and 28 Toast-only rows remain allergen-unavailable with Ingredient Intelligence separate.
The app now labels restaurant-linked menu evidence distinctly instead of presenting it as an official source.

The generic source configuration merged separate Alexandria and Annapolis location menus, admitted six headings or continuation fragments, lost real section context, and omitted many current Alexandria products.
Optional sauces, proteins, and add-ons must not be smeared across every configurable presentation.

The source registry is Alexandria-only; the verified snapshot restores real sections, removes fragments and Annapolis rows, consolidates repeated presentations, places mocktails last, and preserves all current source products.

Optional toppings, sauces, add-ons, and the raw-food advisory do not become fixed allergen claims; the shared classifier has a restaurant-scoped exception for the three current nonalcoholic mocktails it previously rejected.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Accessible restaurant-issued Alexandria menu captured with current products, descriptions, location scope, and global gluten cross-contact warning.
No separate restaurant-issued allergen guide or complete matrix was located.
Live Alexandria Toast catalog identified the 1106 King Street location and supplied a partial set of item-level positive allergen labels; coordinator artifact capture returned HTTP 403.
notes: Toast's generic Allergies modifier is a customer-instruction control, not an item allergen matrix.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Targeted review found the active restaurant-linked ordering surface and no complete allergen matrix.
No material archived allergen source was required because current primary menu evidence was accessible.
A third-party Alexandria listing was found but was not used for fixed allergen claims.

findings.summary: The Alexandria record was built from both location menus and contained nine Annapolis-only rows plus six non-product fragments.
findings.resolution: Scoped configured menu input to Alexandria, removed all fifteen contaminated rows, and retained Annapolis only as audit comparison evidence.

findings.summary: The frozen mixed-location catalog omitted many current Alexandria products and lost real section structure.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt and pinned the full 122-product owner/Toast union across 12 consumer sections, including all current nonalcoholic products.

findings.summary: Every retained frozen row omitted the restaurant-issued Alexandria kitchen-wide gluten cross-contact warning.
findings.resolution: Applied exactly may-contain gluten to every current row without adding wheat or unrelated allergens.

findings.summary: Seven current Toast rows publish positive labels that conflicted with or extended frozen signals.
findings.resolution: Reconciled all seven labels, preserved direct owner terms, ignored missing-label negatives and generic allergy modifiers, and introduced restaurant-linked source types and UI labeling rather than promoting vendor evidence to official.

repairs.summary: Verified the complete 122-product Alexandria catalog, its location scope, source authority, global gluten cross-contact semantics, partial Toast positives, and conservative optional-choice handling.

notes: Terra research: gpt-5.6-terra low; Sol adjudication: gpt-5.6-sol medium; coordinator-strength review corrected both workers' location-scope and cross-contact omissions.
The worker result is ingested only as discrepancy evidence; its proposed ten-row patch and accurately_unavailable grouping are rejected.
Targeted catalog and frozen-reconciliation tests passed: 8/8.
Targeted generated-data regression passed: 1/1.
The first generated regression exposed three verified mocktails being rejected by a generic drink-only classifier; a restaurant-scoped exception now preserves the current nonalcoholic rows.
Targeted audit/reconciliation suite passed 8/8 and the generated-data regression passed 1/1.
Generated snapshot counts: 122 total; 68 restaurant-issued ingredients; 14 official global-note-only; 7 restaurant-linked product-label; 5 restaurant-linked identity; 28 unavailable.
